mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2-year moving average trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable economic expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in quarters above trend are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points triggers a repricing of labor market tightness, reducing wage-inflation fears and boosting equity valuations within the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 2-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS compresses real yields when nominal hikes outpace inflation, signaling effective monetary tightening that dampens equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Real yield compression periods produce positive SPX forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient loses statistical significance (t-stat < 1.96) in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 105 decelerates in late-cycle expansions, indicating supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints that precede earnings downgrades and generate negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 105 consistently correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for SPX momentum over the same window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding the 3-month average signals accelerating income generation, which supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth surprises fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly jump in housing starts exceeding 1300 thousand units acts as a leading indicator for durable goods demand and construction sector profitability, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 1300 correlate with negative SPX forward returns in > 45% of instances, or the signal's predictive rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between accelerating RSAFS and decelerating INDPRO signals demand-driven inflationary pressure without corresponding supply expansion, forcing discount rate hikes that suppress equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Credit expansion paired with flat industrial production yields positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is fully absorbed by SPX trend momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a steepening yield environment where FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL remains flat, real rates increase rapidly, compressing equity duration and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising nominal rates with stable CPI produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance over a 3-year walk-forward test.
